standard_notation: K_{l3}: K -> pi e nu and K -> pi mu nu; |V_us| f_+(0)
process_name: Kaon semileptonic decays and source-level V_us input
implementation_difficulty_reason: A live K018 constraint requires a new charged-current semileptonic observable or EW002 sub-likelihood, radiative/isospin and phase-space conventions, lattice f_+(0) provenance, and BSM charged-current matching. Existing Delta F = 2 operators do not cover it.

evidence: quarkConstraints/scan.py:43 documents PDG-derived global-fit averages including |V_us|.
quarkConstraints/scan.py:247 labels the reduced CKM observable set as Vus, Vcb, Vub, J.
tests/test_quark_fit.py:587 anchors the |V_us| target convention against PDG 2024.
missing: No K_l3, K_e3, K_mu3, |V_us| f_+(0), f_+(0), radiative/isospin correction, or semileptonic-kaon likelihood implementation was found.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 limits the modern phenomenology systems to epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0.

grep_commands.command: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
grep_commands.result_summary: Finds Delta F = 2 neutral-meson and mu->e gamma surfaces only.

grep_commands.command: rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
grep_commands.result_summary: No K_l3 implementation; hits are unrelated Delta F = 2, LFV, QCD, and benchmark surfaces.
Confirms implemented/policy surfaces are neutral-meson Delta F = 2 systems plus mu->e gamma.

grep_commands.command: rg -n "K.?l3|K_l3|Kell3|Ke3|Kmu3|K.?->.?pi.?e.?nu|K.?->.?pi.?mu.?nu|Vus.*f|f_\\+\\(0\\)|semileptonic kaon|Cabibbo|V_us|Vus" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
grep_commands.result_summary: Finds only generic CKM |V_us| fit targets and tests, not K_l3 source observables.

paper_era_reference.observable: 2008-era RS flavor context, not a K_l3 measured observable

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(K_{\ell3}\), with \(K\to\pi e\nu\) and
\(K\to\pi\mu\nu\).  This entry records the kaon semileptonic source input
\(|V_{us}|f_+(0)\) and its mode-by-mode consistency tests.  The first-row CKM
unitarity combination is cataloged separately in EW002.
sections.pdg_prose:
The sidecar \texttt{pdg\_or\_equivalent} entry
\texttt{PDG2025:K018:average\_fplus\_vus}, backed by
\texttt{pdg2025\_vud\_vus\_kl3.txt}, records
\[
  |V_{us}|f_+(0)=0.21656(35),
\]
from the 2025 PDG update, accessed 2026-05-16.  The same sidecar block records
the PDG Table 67.1 products: \(0.2169(6)\) for \(K^\pm e3\),
\(0.2168(10)\) for \(K^\pm\mu3\), \(0.2162(5)\) for \(K_L e3\),
\(0.2165(6)\) for \(K_L\mu3\), \(0.2169(8)\) for \(K_S e3\), and
\(0.2125(47)\) for \(K_S\mu3\).  Additional \texttt{pdg\_or\_equivalent}
entries record the FLAG \(N_f=2+1+1\) normalization \(f_+(0)=0.9698(17)\)
and the PDG-derived \(|V_{us}|=0.22330(53)\); these are normalization inputs,
not separate measured \(K_{\ell3}\) rates.
sections.relevance:
This is a tree-level charged-current process, not a neutral-meson
\(\Delta F=2\) observable.  In the current quark scan it is relevant mainly
as the semileptonic source behind the fitted \(|V_{us}|\) target and the
EW002 first-row unitarity test.  Nonminimal warped models with modified
\(W\)-quark couplings, vector-like fermion mixing, lepton nonuniversality, or
right-handed charged currents could shift the extracted \(V_{us}\) or the
\(K_{\mu3}/K_{e3}\) consistency test, but a minimal KK-gluon flavor analysis
does not map this measurement into the existing operator basis.
sections.post_2008:
The 2008 RS-flavor baseline \texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler:RSFlavor2008}
centered on flavor-changing neutral constraints, so the post-2008 K018 story
is precision charged-current consolidation.  The sidecar entries
\texttt{FlaviaNet2010:K018:average\_fplus\_vus} and
\texttt{FlaviaNet2010:K018:r\_mue} record the 2010 FlaviaNet average
\(|V_{us}|f_+(0)=0.2163(5)\) and \(K_{\mu3}/K_{e3}\) universality ratio
\(r_{\mu e}=1.002(5)\).  The 2025 PDG update keeps the same source-level
convention while tightening the average to \(0.21656(35)\).  FLAG 2024 supplies
the auxiliary lattice normalization, \(f_+(0)=0.9698(17)\), and the derived
\(|V_{us}|=0.22328(58)\), which feed the CKM-unitarity entry rather than
replacing the measured K018 product.
sections.validity:
The measured \(|V_{us}|f_+(0)\) product is experimentally robust but depends
on consistent radiative, isospin, and phase-space conventions.  The derived
\(|V_{us}|\) additionally depends on the lattice \(f_+(0)\) average.  As an
RS constraint, K018 is model-dependent: it is a charged-current CKM and
lepton-universality diagnostic, not a standalone FCNC bound unless the model
specifies nonstandard \(W\), scalar, tensor, or right-handed-current effects.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{PARTIAL.}  The repo has a generic CKM target containing \(|V_{us}|\):
\texttt{quarkConstraints/scan.py:43} documents the PDG-derived CKM averages,
\texttt{quarkConstraints/scan.py:247} labels the reduced CKM observable as
\texttt{Vus}, and \texttt{tests/test\_quark\_fit.py:587} anchors the target
against PDG 2024.  The coverage grep found no \(K_{\ell3}\) decay-rate,
\(|V_{us}|f_+(0)\), \(f_+(0)\), \(K_{e3}\), or \(K_{\mu3}\) implementation.
The modern flavor surface is still limited to \(\varepsilon_K\), \(K\),
\(B_d\), \(B_s\), and \(D0\) systems at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  A live K018 constraint would need a charged-current
semileptonic likelihood or EFT wrapper, radiative/isospin convention
bookkeeping, lattice \(f_+(0)\) provenance, and a decision about whether it is
implemented as a direct \(V_{us}\) target, an EW002 sub-input, or a BSM
charged-current operator constraint.  The existing \(\Delta F=2\)
SLL/SLR/VLL/VRR/LR machinery does not cover it.
